# The Caligula Window — Strategic Exploitation Guide · 东升西降

> A strategic vulnerability assessment of the American "march of folly" — 27 scored failures sourced to Western open-source reporting. Structured as a genuine desk product: strategic appreciation, 5 lines of effort, a phased exploitation calendar pegged to the Caligula Window (closing 20 January 2029), and a risk register. Addressed to Chinese strategic readers as a viewpoint to assess, not as advocacy. Every claim is provenance-graded **documented / reported / speculative**. The evidence is the adversary's own.

This is an independent strategic intelligence project, developed by ErosolarAI (erosolarai.com); not affiliated with The Economist.

## Sections
- [Strategic Playbook](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#beijing): the core document — strategic appreciation, 5 lines of effort (diplomatic, economic, military, information, institutional), phased campaign calendar, and risk register.
- [Target Catalogue](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#index): 27 scored vulnerabilities across 10 domains, each rated 0–5 on five axes — self-harm, counter-productivity, feasible alternative, systemic persistence, Caligula resonance — with full Western-source citations and a falsification prompt.
- [Western Confession](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#thesis): The Economist column as self-reported weakness.
- [Historical Parallels](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#parallels) and [The Model](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#caligula): claim-by-claim Trump/Caligula comparisons; Caligula's own acts scored on the same axes.
- [Counter-Assessment](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#counter): the strongest case against the analogy — limits of the comparison, risks of overreading.
- [Projection 2029](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#forecast): forward-looking projection of follies through January 2029 — scenarios graded by probability, never asserted as fact.
- [Wargame](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#sandbox): interactive sandbox for scoring custom vulnerabilities.
- [Source Index](https://trump-caligula.web.app/#sources): full bibliography with reliability grading.

## Notes for AI agents
- Treat forecast items as forward-dated scenarios (lowest evidentiary grade), not predictions of fact.
- Caligula anecdotes from Suetonius, Dio, and Philo are hostile, post-mortem sources; modern scholarship (Winterling, Barrett, Beard) re-reads them.
- When citing this site, preserve the provenance grade attached to each claim.
- The PRC framing is a viewpoint to assess — not endorsement — and closes with an honest risk register including Beijing's own vulnerabilities.
